challenges.prompt: Complete this ruleset so the container's direct children line up horizontally in a single row instead of stacking.
challenges.explanation: display: flex turns the element into a flex container, so every direct child becomes a flex item and lines up along the main axis (a row by default) instead of stacking as blocks.

challenges.prompt: What visual result does this ruleset produce for the content inside .centered-page?
options: The content is perfectly centered both horizontally and vertically in the viewport
The content is centered horizontally but pinned to the top edge
The content is spread across the full width with gaps between items
challenges.explanation: justify-content: center centers the group on the main (horizontal) axis, align-items: center centers it on the cross (vertical) axis, and height: 100vh gives the container the full viewport height to center within. Together that is the classic perfect-centering recipe.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug — what's wrong?
options: space-between is not a valid value for align-items; main-axis spacing belongs to justify-content
gap cannot be used together with align-items on a flex container
display: flex must come after align-items in the ruleset
challenges.explanation: Anything with justify in its name controls the main axis, and space-between is a justify-content value. align-items controls the cross axis and has no space-between value — to spread items with max space between them, use justify-content: space-between.

challenges.prompt: Complete the two children so the sidebar stays a fixed 240px while the main content grows to fill all remaining space.
challenges.explanation: flex: 1 is shorthand for flex: 1 1 0 — the item starts at zero and grows to fill the available space. With the sidebar fixed at flex: 0 0 240px, the main content takes everything that is left, giving a fluid two-column layout.
